tasks.context: S1.2 frozen question: can five simple, distinct weapon packages make four independently firing mounts readable and replayable while preserving unlimited normal fire, independent power banks, numeric-only empowerment, movement quality, and the accepted ten-day cap?
tasks.title: Define the amended Stage 1 weapon-package baseline
tasks.summary: Define and validate the five-package authoring boundary and numeric-only empowered-profile rules.
tasks.objective: Create the package descriptor and validator consumed by all Stage 1 weapon packages without changing the accepted combat contracts or central registry generator.
inputs: Consumed contracts: StableId v1, ContentVersion v1, Typed Content References v1, Generated Registry v1, WeaponRuntimeProfile, independent power-bank policy, and modular weapon-behavior pipeline.
Accepted roster and empowered-fire boundaries from AMENDMENT_STAGE1_WEAPONS.md.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: accepted intake/requirements/architecture outside the amendment, combat-foundation files, central generated registry outputs, scenes, UI, enemies, mission state, saves, rewards, Packages, ProjectSettings, networking, and backend code.

Serialized ownership: none. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: contributes validated descriptor inputs only; Assets/ShooterMover/Generated outputs remain exclusively CS-011-owned and are regenerated, never edited here.
Rollback: remove the four owned files before package consumers land. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: Exactly the five amended stable IDs are accepted and the Blaster Machine Gun can be marked as the default starting weapon.
Normal fire cannot declare consumable ammunition and every empowered profile is limited to existing numeric coefficients.
Arc profiles reject more than three additional targets, Ricochet profiles reject more than two wall bounces, and Rocket profiles reject fragmentation or second-detonation behavior.
Unknown, duplicate, malformed, or behavior-changing package descriptors fail deterministically.
verification: Automated: run descriptor identity, duplicate-ID, numeric empowerment, Arc cap, Ricochet cap, Rocket topology, and unlimited-normal-fire tests.
Manual/playable: contract review confirms each of the five approved packages fits without modifying CB-004 or adding a universal weapon switch.
proof_required: accepted and intentionally rejected descriptor fixtures
edge_cases: empowered profile attempts to change module topology
tasks.handoff_notes: WP-002 through WP-007 consume this baseline; CS-011 remains the sole central generated-output owner.

tasks.context: S1.2 frozen question: can five simple, distinct weapon packages make four independently firing mounts readable and replayable while preserving unlimited normal fire, independent power banks, numeric-only empowerment, movement quality, and the accepted ten-day cap?
tasks.title: Create bounded shared projectile and hit-presentation primitives
tasks.summary: Provide the minimal package-owned 2D projectile shell and temporary hit hook reused by the five weapon packages.
tasks.objective: Implement only the concrete 2D projectile lifetime, collision handoff and temporary hit-presentation primitives needed by Stage 1 packages behind the accepted execution-plan adapter.
inputs: Consumed contracts: Combat Messages v1, WeaponFireExecutionPlan, WeaponMount2DAdapter boundary, and Stage 1 weapon-package baseline.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: CB-owned pipeline/adapters, central registries, movement files, scenes, UI, package-specific folders, enemies, mission state, saves, rewards, Packages, ProjectSettings, 3D physics, and generalized pooling frameworks.
Serialized ownership: exclusive ownership of BoundedProjectile2D.prefab. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: descriptor input only; no generated output edit.
Rollback: remove the five owned files; no accepted contract changes. Prototype debt: none; the bounded primitive must meet Stage 1 performance evidence without speculative generalization.
acceptance_criteria: The primitive uses only Unity 2D APIs, has finite validated lifetime, reports confirmed hits once, and cannot become authoritative combat state.
Execution originates only from a validated fire plan and package-specific behavior remains outside the shared primitive.
Temporary hit presentation is optional, bounded and does not carry damage or target-selection authority.
verification: Automated: play-mode tests cover spawn, finite expiry, one-hit translation, disabled presentation, rapid restart and invalid-plan rejection.
Manual/playable: inspect the prefab and confirm no package identity, scene reference, 3D component or hidden global service is embedded.
tasks.handoff_notes: Five package tasks may proceed in parallel after this shared primitive and WP-001 are accepted.

tasks.context: S1.2 frozen question: can five simple, distinct weapon packages make four independently firing mounts readable and replayable while preserving unlimited normal fire, independent power banks, numeric-only empowerment, movement quality, and the accepted ten-day cap?
tasks.title: Build the Blaster Machine Gun package
tasks.summary: Create the default straightforward automatic-fire reference weapon.
tasks.objective: Implement the Blaster Machine Gun as the default starting package with simple automatic projectile fire and numeric-only empowerment.
inputs: Consumed contracts: Four-Mount Weapons v1 and Combat Messages v1; plus the accepted four-mount coordinator, Stage 1 package baseline, and shared projectile primitive.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: every other weapon package, shared primitive, combat foundation, generated outputs, scenes, central UI, enemies, mission state, saves, rewards, Packages and ProjectSettings.

Serialized ownership: every asset under BlasterMachineGun only. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: contributes weapon.blaster-machine-gun descriptor input; generated outputs remain CS-011-owned.
Rollback: remove the owned package folder and test file. Prototype debt: temporary presentation is supplied by WP-010, not duplicated here.
acceptance_criteria: The stable ID is weapon.blaster-machine-gun and the descriptor marks it as the default starting weapon.
Holding fire produces straightforward independent automatic fire with no special secondary behavior or consumable normal ammo.
Empowerment changes authored numeric values only and empty power falls back immediately to identical normal-fire topology.
verification: Automated: play-mode cadence, aim, unlimited normal fire, independent power fallback, restart and stable-identity tests.
Manual/playable: confirm this reads as the uncomplicated baseline beside the other four packages.
tasks.handoff_notes: May run concurrently with WP-004 through WP-007; never edit their package folders.

tasks.context: S1.2 frozen question: can five simple, distinct weapon packages make four independently firing mounts readable and replayable while preserving unlimited normal fire, independent power banks, numeric-only empowerment, movement quality, and the accepted ten-day cap?
tasks.title: Build the Shotgun package
tasks.summary: Create the close-range multi-projectile spread weapon.
tasks.objective: Implement deterministic bounded spread fire with package-owned pellet tuning and numeric-only empowerment.
inputs: Consumed contracts: Four-Mount Weapons v1 and Combat Messages v1; plus the accepted behavior pipeline, coordinator, package baseline, and projectile primitive.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: other weapon packages, shared primitive, combat foundation, generated outputs, scenes, central UI, enemies, mission state, saves, rewards, Packages and ProjectSettings.
Serialized ownership: every asset under Shotgun only. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: contributes weapon.shotgun descriptor input only.
Rollback: remove the owned package folder and test file. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: One fire plan emits a deterministically ordered, bounded pellet spread with no duplicate damage event per confirmed pellet.
Normal fire is unlimited and independent; empowerment only tunes existing numeric spread, damage, cadence, speed or recovery values.
Pellet count and spread bounds prevent unbounded projectile density.
verification: Automated: pellet order/count, spread bounds, close aim, independent fallback, restart and density-budget tests.
Manual/playable: confirm clear close-range identity without hiding enemy telegraphs.
tasks.handoff_notes: May run concurrently with WP-003 and WP-005 through WP-007.

tasks.context: S1.2 frozen question: can five simple, distinct weapon packages make four independently firing mounts readable and replayable while preserving unlimited normal fire, independent power banks, numeric-only empowerment, movement quality, and the accepted ten-day cap?
tasks.title: Build the Rocket Launcher package
tasks.summary: Create the paced projectile weapon with one simple bounded area detonation.
tasks.objective: Implement a deterministic rocket impact/expiry detonation using the accepted combat messages and numeric-only empowerment.
inputs: Consumed contracts: Combat Messages v1 and Four-Mount Weapons v1; plus the accepted behavior pipeline, coordinator, package baseline, and projectile primitive.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: other weapon packages, shared primitive, combat foundation, generated outputs, scenes, central UI, enemies, mission state, saves, rewards, Packages and ProjectSettings.
Serialized ownership: every asset under RocketLauncher only. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: contributes weapon.rocket-launcher descriptor input only.
Rollback: remove the owned package folder and test file. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: A rocket produces at most one deterministic bounded area detonation on valid impact or authored expiry.
Normal fire is unlimited; empowerment only tunes existing numeric values such as damage, speed, radius, cadence or recovery.
No fragmentation, second detonation, persistent area, homing or scene-owned damage state is introduced.
verification: Automated: single-detonation, radius boundary, stable target ordering, impact/expiry race, fallback and restart tests.
Manual/playable: confirm readable area warning and impact without excessive screen obstruction.
tasks.handoff_notes: May run concurrently with WP-003, WP-004, WP-006 and WP-007.

tasks.context: S1.2 frozen question: can five simple, distinct weapon packages make four independently firing mounts readable and replayable while preserving unlimited normal fire, independent power banks, numeric-only empowerment, movement quality, and the accepted ten-day cap?
tasks.title: Build the Arc Gun package
tasks.summary: Create the original bounded chain-lightning weapon.
tasks.objective: Implement deterministic nearby-target chaining from one primary hit to at most three additional eligible targets with numeric-only empowerment.
inputs: Consumed contracts: Combat Messages v1 and Four-Mount Weapons v1; plus the accepted behavior pipeline, coordinator, package baseline, and shared hit-presentation hook.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: other weapon packages, shared primitive, combat foundation, generated outputs, scenes, central UI, enemies, mission state, saves, rewards, third-party SAS assets/code, Packages and ProjectSettings.
Serialized ownership: every asset under ArcGun only. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: contributes weapon.arc-gun descriptor input only.
Rollback: remove the owned package folder and two test files. Prototype debt: none; naming, code, tuning and presentation must be original.
acceptance_criteria: A primary hit may chain to at most three additional nearby eligible targets and never hits one target twice in the same chain.
Distance ties resolve deterministically by stable target identity and dead, invalid, out-of-range or already-visited targets are skipped.
Empowerment may tune damage, range, cadence or other existing numeric coefficients but never increases the three-additional-target cap or changes topology.
verification: Automated: zero-to-three chain, fourth-target rejection, stable tie-break, disappearing target, no-repeat, fallback and restart tests.
Manual/playable: confirm the chain path is readable, original in presentation and distinguishable amid four-mount fire.
tasks.handoff_notes: May run concurrently with WP-003 through WP-005 and WP-007.

tasks.context: S1.2 frozen question: can five simple, distinct weapon packages make four independently firing mounts readable and replayable while preserving unlimited normal fire, independent power banks, numeric-only empowerment, movement quality, and the accepted ten-day cap?
tasks.title: Build the Ricochet Gun package
tasks.summary: Create the long-lived projectile weapon capped at two wall bounces.
tasks.objective: Implement deterministic 2D wall reflection, finite lifetime and expiry after at most two authored wall bounces with numeric-only empowerment.
inputs: Consumed contracts: Combat Messages v1 and Four-Mount Weapons v1; plus the accepted 2D baseline, behavior pipeline, coordinator, Unity 2D execution boundary, package baseline, and projectile primitive.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: movement wall-reflection ownership, other weapon packages, shared primitive, combat foundation, generated outputs, scenes, central UI, enemies, mission state, saves, rewards, 3D physics, Packages and ProjectSettings.
Serialized ownership: every asset under RicochetGun only. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: contributes weapon.ricochet-gun descriptor input only.
Rollback: remove the owned package folder and two test files. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: Projectiles have a long but finite validated lifetime, reflect deterministically from valid 2D walls, and perform at most two wall bounces.
After the second bounce, the next wall collision or lifetime expiry terminates the projectile without another reflection.
Empowerment may tune existing numeric values but never increases the two-bounce cap or creates persistent projectiles.
verification: Automated: first/second bounce, third-collision expiry, corner collision, grazing contact, lifetime expiry, fallback and restart tests.
Manual/playable: confirm bank shots are predictable and projectiles remain readable without accumulating indefinitely.
tasks.handoff_notes: May run concurrently with WP-003 through WP-006; do not edit MT-005 files.

tasks.context: S1.2 frozen question: can five simple, distinct weapon packages make four independently firing mounts readable and replayable while preserving unlimited normal fire, independent power banks, numeric-only empowerment, movement quality, and the accepted ten-day cap?
tasks.title: Define deterministic Stage 1 loadout fixtures
tasks.summary: Create reproducible four-slot comparisons and the non-persistent five-weapon evidence wrapper.
tasks.objective: Provide fixed four-slot loadouts that include the default Blaster Machine Gun and deterministic seeded selection from exactly the five approved packages.
inputs: Consumed contracts: Four-Mount Weapons v1 and BuildIdentity/ContentVersion v1; plus the deterministic evidence seed fixture and all five package descriptors.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: package implementation folders, central registries, runtime inventory/economy, saves, rewards, shops, scenes, UI, enemies, Packages and ProjectSettings.

Serialized ownership: every asset under Stage1Loadouts and the fixture directory only. Save-schema impact: none; wrapper state is evidence-session-only. Content-registry impact: loadout descriptor inputs only.
Rollback: remove the three owned areas. Prototype debt: evidence-only randomized wrapper expires when Stage 2 inventory/loadout state becomes authoritative.
acceptance_criteria: Every fixture has exactly four stable ordered slots and resolves only the five approved package IDs.
The default comparison includes the Blaster Machine Gun; alternate fixtures expose all five identities across the matrix.
The seeded wrapper is reproducible, cannot persist rewards or inventory and never creates randomized weapon modifiers.
verification: Automated: slot count/order, unknown ID, seed reproducibility, all-five coverage and no-persistence tests.
Manual/playable: human reviewer can select documented fixed comparisons without editing serialized scenes.
tasks.handoff_notes: WP-011 consumes these fixtures; production loadout ownership remains Stage 2 work.

tasks.context: S1.2 frozen question: can five simple, distinct weapon packages make four independently firing mounts readable and replayable while preserving unlimited normal fire, independent power banks, numeric-only empowerment, movement quality, and the accepted ten-day cap?
tasks.title: Validate five weapon packages and registry inputs
tasks.summary: Add a deterministic package matrix that proves all five descriptors and references are valid without editing generated registry outputs.
tasks.objective: Exercise the CS-011 generator/validator against package inputs, detect drift and verify power/four-mount invariants across the amended roster.
inputs: Consumed contracts: Typed Content References v1, Generated Registry v1 and Four-Mount Weapons v1; plus the sole registry generator/validator, power-bank policy, coordinator, and all five package inputs.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: Assets/ShooterMover/Generated outputs, CS-011 generator code, package implementation folders, scenes, UI, enemies, mission state, saves, rewards, Packages and ProjectSettings.
Serialized ownership: none. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: validation-only; regenerate to a temporary test location and never commit or hand-edit CS-011-owned outputs.
Rollback: remove the three owned files. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: Exactly five unique weapon descriptors validate and every reference resolves with deterministic ordering.
Generator drift, duplicate IDs, unknown modules, invalid empowered profiles and stale package inputs fail visibly.
The committed central generated outputs are unchanged by this task.
verification: Automated: run package matrix twice with shuffled inputs, compare checksums, inject drift and confirm failure.
Manual/playable: reviewer verifies the diff contains no Assets/ShooterMover/Generated changes.
tasks.handoff_notes: Any committed central-output regeneration remains a separately reviewed CS-011-owner action.

tasks.context: S1.2 frozen question: can five simple, distinct weapon packages make four independently firing mounts readable and replayable while preserving unlimited normal fire, independent power banks, numeric-only empowerment, movement quality, and the accepted ten-day cap?
tasks.title: Add the minimal weapon-status strip and temporary audiovisual identity
tasks.summary: Create bounded Stage 1 presentation that distinguishes five weapons and communicates four independent mount states.
tasks.objective: Consume the immutable CB-010 snapshot to present readiness, power/fallback and package identity with color-independent temporary HUD, effects and audio prioritization.
inputs: Consumed contracts: Four-Mount HUD v1 and Four-Mount Weapons v1; plus the immutable four-mount status snapshot and package presentation references.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: CB-010 projector, central/global UI systems, package behavior files, generated registries, scenes, enemies, mission state, saves, rewards, final-art pipeline, Packages and ProjectSettings.

Serialized ownership: every prefab, ScriptableObject, temporary audio/effect and UI asset under Stage1Presentation only. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: presentation references only.
Rollback: remove the owned folder and test file; combat state remains valid. Prototype debt: PD-WP-001 records temporary HUD/effects/audio, expiring before Stage 2 representative-art acceptance.
acceptance_criteria: Exactly four stable slots show readiness, recovery, power level, normal/empowered/fallback state and fault state without reading mutable combat state.
All five weapons are distinguishable through at least two cues and critical state never depends on color alone.
Empowered use, expenditure, depletion and fallback are unmistakable even though empowerment is numeric-only.
Audio/effect priority remains bounded so enemy warnings can remain dominant.
verification: Automated: snapshot projection, slot order, missing reference, reduced-effects fixture, power fallback and bounded-priority tests.
Manual/playable: human inspection confirms identity, color-independent status and warning headroom at four-mount effect density.
tasks.handoff_notes: WP-012 performs the formal human identity/readability gate; later UI work may replace presentation without changing combat state.

tasks.context: S1.2 frozen question: can five simple, distinct weapon packages make four independently firing mounts readable and replayable while preserving unlimited normal fire, independent power banks, numeric-only empowerment, movement quality, and the accepted ten-day cap?
tasks.title: Add representative five-weapon evidence scenarios
tasks.summary: Replace synthetic-only combat evidence with reproducible scenarios covering all five packages and representative four-slot combinations.
tasks.objective: Use the existing benchmark arena read-only to verify package identity, numeric empowerment, independent fallback, caps, restart, diagnostics and performance.
inputs: Consumed contracts: BuildIdentity/ContentVersion v1, Diagnostics and Run Validity v1, Combat Messages v1 and Four-Mount Weapons v1; plus the evidence configuration/arena/lifecycle/performance/manifests/smoke/protocol, movement evidence, combat foundation evidence, loadout fixtures, package validation and presentation.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: EH-004 benchmark scene, package implementation folders, central generated registries, shared evidence frameworks, movement/combat foundation, enemies, mission state, saves, rewards, Packages and ProjectSettings.
Serialized ownership: exclusive ownership of stage1-five-weapon-evidence-v1.json only; benchmark scene is read-only. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
Rollback: remove the three owned files; package tests remain valid. Prototype debt: evidence fixture is Stage 1-only and expires after the formal Stage 1 gate.
acceptance_criteria: Frozen scenarios exercise every weapon and at least two reproducible four-slot loadouts with one shared aim/fire stream.
Arc never exceeds three additional targets, Ricochet never exceeds two bounces, Rocket detonates once, and all empowered modes preserve normal behavior topology.
Mixed depletion falls back independently, restart reproduces initial state, diagnostics/manifests identify all packages, and invalid sessions are classified.
Representative effect/projectile density remains inside recorded Stage 1 performance budgets.
verification: Automated: run edit/play/smoke entrypoints for identity, caps, fallback, restart, diagnostics, manifest checksum and performance-budget scenarios.
Manual/playable: prepare a review build and evidence package for WP-012 without claiming human quality acceptance.
proof_required: valid and intentionally invalid manifested evidence packages
tasks.handoff_notes: WP-012 is the explicit human acceptance dependency for weapon identity, audiovisual feedback and readability.

tasks.context: S1.2 frozen question: can five simple, distinct weapon packages make four independently firing mounts readable and replayable while preserving unlimited normal fire, independent power banks, numeric-only empowerment, movement quality, and the accepted ten-day cap?
tasks.title: Record the human weapon-identity and readability gate
tasks.summary: Run and record the explicit human playable review required before downstream Stage 1 work treats the weapon set as accepted.
tasks.objective: Evaluate movement-preserving feel, shared aiming, five weapon identities, numeric empowerment feedback, audiovisual priority and four-slot readability using the immutable WP-011 evidence package.
inputs: Consumed contracts: Diagnostics and Run Validity v1 and BuildIdentity/ContentVersion v1; plus the Stage 1 evidence protocol and immutable five-weapon evidence package.
outputs: Machine-readable pass/fail checklist and bounded findings
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: all gameplay/content implementation, tuning assets, scenes, prefabs, ScriptableObjects, generated registries, evidence framework, enemies, mission state, saves, rewards, Packages and ProjectSettings.
Serialized ownership: none. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
Rollback: remove only the two review artifacts and return the batch to blocked review. Prototype debt: records PD-WP-001 disposition but cannot waive unresolved blocking debt.
This task records evidence only; any required implementation change becomes a separately reviewed bounded follow-up and may not be hidden inside the review commit.
acceptance_criteria: The human lead records pass/fail for aiming, each weapon's identity, normal/power distinction, fallback, movement interaction, impact feedback, warning headroom and reduced-effects readability.
Arc chaining and Ricochet bounces are judged predictable; Rocket area damage and Shotgun density remain readable; Blaster remains a clear default baseline.
Every blocking finding names affected evidence and required bounded follow-up; no failed quality dimension is silently accepted.
The review states whether Stage 1 enemy/route work may consume the five-weapon set.
verification: Automated: validate checklist JSON shape, required dimensions, build/content/tuning fingerprints and WP-011 evidence links before accepting the record.
Manual/playable: complete the frozen checklist across the documented fixed loadouts and reduced-effects profile on the identified build.
Review: verify human identity, date, build/content/tuning fingerprints and links to WP-011 proof are present.
edge_cases: one weapon indistinguishable under four-mount load
tasks.handoff_notes: Downstream Stage 1 tasks must depend on this recorded human gate when claiming weapon feel, identity, audiovisual feedback or readability acceptance.
tasks.notes: Milestone-cap spend: 0.40 focused lead day. Batch direct spend totals 4.60 focused lead days, leaving 0.90 of the S1.2 ten-day cap for review reserve and bounded approved follow-up.
